name: Ph.D. in Information Systems

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The only parsed content is IS 791 Doctoral Seminar (0 credits, requirementGroups 0), so no coursework spine, breadth requirement, research-methods sequence, or qualifying-exam-to-dissertation progression is visible; even by dissertation-driven doctoral norms the page exposes essentially no intellectual pathway.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a structured plan of study showing required coursework (IS theory and research-methods seminars), qualifying/comprehensive exams, and dissertation milestones so progression is visible beyond a single seminar.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: metrics show deepestPrereqChain 0 and missingCourseCount 0, so there are no dangling references (clean), but with only one 0-depth seminar there is no prerequisite structure to evaluate; a flat graph is defensible for an advisor-gated doctorate but conveys nothing about expected preparation.
dimensions.suggestion: Document expected prerequisite competencies or bridge coursework for entrants from non-IS backgrounds so the on-ramp to doctoral study is explicit.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or doctoral research competencies are present in the data, a real gap for a catalog page where outcomes normally frame the qualifying exam, dissertation, and expected scholarly contributions.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish doctoral learning outcomes (independent research design, IS theory mastery, dissemination and teaching competencies) tied to program milestones.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null and creditReconcileDelta is null, so the page shows no total-credit requirement and no coursework-versus-dissertation split; while variable dissertation credit is expected for a Ph.D., the complete absence of any credit figure leaves the credential opaque.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the total post-baccalaureate credit requirement and the split between required coursework, electives, and dissertation research.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page renders as a single 0-credit seminar (IS 791) with no program overview, research areas, faculty clusters, or milestone timeline; there is nothing structured for a prospective doctoral student to read.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program overview with research concentrations, faculty research clusters, and a milestone timeline (coursework, exams, proposal, dissertation).

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: As a research doctorate its currency lives in its research frontier, but with only IS 791 visible there is no evidence of engagement with current IS scholarship such as AI/ML in organizations, digital platforms, or cybersecurity/privacy economics.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface research concentrations and signature courses (e.g., AI/ML in IS, digital platforms and ecosystems, healthcare informatics, security/privacy economics) to convey current research strengths.

descriptionQuality.justification: The single parsed course, IS 791 Doctoral Seminar, has an adequate and specific description covering advanced-topic presentations, research procedures, and dissertation organization, but with n=1 there is no basis to judge the program's course descriptions broadly.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: IS 791 lists no prerequisite and missingCourseCount is 0, so there are no dangling or inconsistent references; however, with a single seminar there is little to assess, and no gating (advisor permission, exam status) is stated.

missingTopics.topic: Research methods sequence (quantitative, qualitative, design science)
missingTopics.rationale: A doctoral core needs explicit methods training, but only IS 791 seminar is documented with no methods course visible.

missingTopics.topic: Econometrics and causal inference for IS research
missingTopics.rationale: Empirical IS scholarship depends on causal and econometric methods that are absent from the parsed content.
Doctoral students need grounding in IS theory (adoption, socio-technical systems, IT value), which no listed course provides.

missingTopics.topic: Data science and machine learning methods for IS
missingTopics.rationale: Modern IS research increasingly uses ML and large-scale data methods that do not appear in the program's courses.
Responsible-conduct-of-research and publication practice are standard doctoral requirements not evidenced here.

emergingTrends.trend: Generative AI and LLMs in organizations
emergingTrends.rationale: How firms adopt, govern, and derive value from LLMs is a leading IS research question the doctorate should position for.
Platform competition, network effects, and governance are a dominant IS research strand that should anchor a concentration.
Fairness and accountability of automated systems is a fast-growing IS/ethics frontier for dissertation work.

emergingTrends.trend: Human-AI collaboration and future of work
emergingTrends.rationale: AI augmentation of knowledge work is reshaping organizations and is a natural doctoral research focus.

emergingTrends.trend: Computational social science on large behavioral data
emergingTrends.rationale: Large-scale digital-trace data enable new IS inquiry and require dedicated methodological training.

topSuggestions: Publish a full plan of study (coursework, qualifying/comprehensive exams, proposal, dissertation) so the page shows a real curriculum beyond the doctoral seminar.
State doctoral learning outcomes and research competencies tied to program milestones.
Publish total credit requirements and the coursework-versus-dissertation credit split.
Add an explicit research-methods and data-science/ML methods sequence to the doctoral core.
Surface research concentrations and signature faculty courses to convey research currency.
